name: Arcosa, Inc.
business: Arcosa, Inc. is a provider of infrastructure-related products and solutions with leading brands serving construction, engineered structures, and transportation markets in North America. The company's three segments are Construction Products (natural and recycled aggregates, specialty materials, asphalt, and construction site support equipment), Engineered Structures (utility structures, wind towers, traffic and lighting structures, and telecommunication structures), and Transportation Products (inland barges, fiberglass barge covers, winches, and marine hardware).
sellsTo: public infrastructure and private construction (Construction Products)
wind turbine producers (Engineered Structures wind towers)
state Departments of Transportation (traffic and lighting structures)
commercial marine transportation companies, lessors, and industrial shippers (Transportation Products inland barges)

exposures.sentence: investments in grid-hardening and connecting renewables to the grid
the expansion of data centers and rise in electricity consumption
investments in grid-hardening and connecting renewables to the grid
we expect to benefit from continued spending on the buildout of 5G and other wireless networks in North America within our telecommunication structures business.
the replacement and growth of aging transportation infrastructure

Over a multi-year period, we believe that approximately 45% of our Construction Products segment revenues are used for infrastructure construction, 25% for non-residential construction, 20% for residential construction, and the remainder for specialty and other end markets.

Our specialty materials, including lightweight aggregates, select natural aggregates, and milled or processed specialty building products and agricultural products, are produced and distributed nationwide.
Our utility structures business is well-positioned to benefit from significant investment in utility infrastructure.

Demand for non-residential construction is driven primarily by population and economic growth, in addition to segment-specific factors such as the growth of e-commerce and artificial intelligence ("AI"), changes in retail patterns, changes in office occupancy trends, financing costs, and numerous other factors.

catalysts.sentence: On August 16, 2024, the Company completed the divestiture of its steel components business.

catalysts.event: Announcement of agreement to sell inland barge and marine components business
catalysts.sentence: on February 24, 2026, we announced an agreement to sell our inland barge and marine components business which is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.
On October 1, 2024, we completed the acquisition of the construction materials business of Stavola Holding Corporation and its affiliated entities ("Stavola") for $1.2 billion in cash.

In April 2024, the Company completed the acquisition of Ameron Pole Products, LLC ("Ameron"), a leading manufacturer of highly engineered, premium concrete, and steel poles for a broad range of infrastructure applications, including lighting, traffic, electric distribution, and small-cell telecom, for $180.0 million in cash.

catalysts.event: Conversion of idled wind tower facility to utility structures expected operational
catalysts.sentence: we are currently in the process of converting an idled wind tower facility to utility structures, which is expected to be operational in the second-half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Enactment of One Big Beautiful Bill Act
catalysts.sentence: The One Big Beautiful Bill Act ( OBBBA ), which was enacted on July 4, 2025, includes several provisions that roll-back, phase out, repeal, and/or add stricter eligibility requirements for, several tax incentives applicable to wind and solar projects.

tamClaims.claim: IIJA provides approximately $350 billion for federal highway programs from 2022 through 2026
tamClaims.sentence: On November 15, 2021, the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act ( IIJA ) was signed into law, which provides approximately $350 billion for federal highway programs from 2022 through 2026 by extending many of the programs in the FAST Act at higher funding levels, as well as supplemental funding for roads, bridges, and other major projects.

tamClaims.claim: FAST Act authorized $305 billion of public infrastructure funding from 2016 to 2020
tamClaims.sentence: The FAST Act authorized $305 billion of public infrastructure funding from 2016 to 2020.

tamClaims.claim: TxDOT 2026 fiscal year planned lettings of approximately $10.9 billion
tamClaims.sentence: The outlook for construction spending in Texas is stable with 2026 fiscal year planned Texas Department of Transportation ( TxDOT ) lettings of approximately $10.9 billion.
The outlook for infrastructure spending in New Jersey is stable with a New Jersey Department of Transportation ( NJDOT ) 2026 budget of $3.6 billion, reflecting an increase of 14.8% from prior year.

tamClaims.claim: NJTA approved a $2.8 billion budget for 2026
tamClaims.sentence: Additionally, the New Jersey Turnpike Authority ( NJTA ) approved a $2.8 billion budget for 2026, a 3.7% increase from prior year.
